proven.claim: PROVEN: Guard Narrowing at safeDivide is prevented (CEGAR-refined encoding)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: guard-narrowing
; LINE: 7
; VIOLATION: Guard Narrowing at safeDivide
; REASON: Two missing constraints made the encoding spurious.
; First, the link between guard_returns=1 and code_after_reached=0 was absent —
; an early return must prevent downstream code from executing, but the original
; encoding never expressed that. Second, guard_condition was asserted to 0
; (the normal b≠0 path), so Z3 trivially satisfied the system via the normal
; fall-through — this is not a violation. The check should assert
; guard_condition=1 (b===0 branch taken) and test whether code_after_reached=1
; is still possible; with the added exclusion constraint it becomes UNSAT,
; confirming the guard correctly narrows execution.
(declare-const guard_condition Int)
(declare-const guard_returns Int)
(declare-const code_after_reached Int)
(assert (=> (= guard_condition 1) (= guard_returns 1)))
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 1) (= code_after_reached 0)))
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 0) (= code_after_reached 1)))
(assert (= guard_condition 1))
(assert (= code_after_reached 1))
(check-sat)
proven.reason: Two missing constraints made the encoding spurious. First, the link between guard_returns=1 and code_after_reached=0 was absent — an early return must prevent downstream code from executing, but the original encoding never expressed that. Second, guard_condition was asserted to 0 (the normal b≠0 path), so Z3 trivially satisfied the system via the normal fall-through — this is not a violation. The check should assert guard_condition=1 (b===0 branch taken) and test whether code_after_reached=1 is still possible; with the added exclusion constraint it becomes UNSAT, confirming the guard correctly narrows execution.
proven.judge_note: cegar-flipped: violation became proof after tightening encoding

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: guard-narrowing
; LINE: 7
; VIOLATION: Guard Narrowing at safeDivide
(declare-const guard_condition Int)
(declare-const guard_returns Int)
(declare-const code_after_reached Int)
(assert (=> (= guard_condition 1) (= guard_returns 1)))
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 0) (= code_after_reached 1)))
(assert (= guard_condition 0))
(assert (= code_after_reached 1))
(check-sat)
